content.subject: english
content.title: Understanding Bicycles
content.concept: Learning about bicycles and their parts.
content.explanation: A bicycle is a fun way to move around! It has parts like wheels, pedals, and a bell. 🚲

practice_questions.q: What colour is the seat of the bicycle?
What do you need to keep the bicycle moving?

content.subject: english
content.title: Bicycle Rhyme
content.content:
I have a red bicycle.
I pedal and I ride.
Trin... trin... trin... goes the bell.
Makes my friends move aside.
The seat is blue in colour,
The tyres and pedals are black.
Sometimes fast, sometimes slow,
But my bicycle is always on track.
To keep my bicycle moving,
All I need is some air.
I put on my little helmet,
And go happily everywhere.

content.subject: english
content.title: Identify Bicycle Parts
content.concept: Recognizing different parts of a bicycle.
content.explanation: Can you name the parts of a bicycle? Let's write them down! 📝

content.subject: english
content.title: Imagine Your Magic Bicycle
content.concept: Creative thinking about a magic bicycle.
content.explanation: Imagine if you had a magic bicycle! Where would you go? 🌈

content.subject: english
content.title: Fill in the Blanks
content.explanation: Let's complete the sentences about the bicycle! ✏️

practice_questions.q: The child in the poem has a __________ bicycle.
The sound of the _______ makes people move aside.
The __________ and __________ are black.

content.subject: english
content.title: Understanding Pictures
content.concept: Learning to observe and describe pictures.
content.explanation: Look at the picture and answer questions about it. What do you see? How many children are there? What are they doing? 🤔
examples: What do you see in the picture?
How are the children having fun?

practice_questions.q: How many children are there in the picture?
practice_questions.a: Count the children in the picture.

practice_questions.q: What games do you play?

content.subject: english
content.title: OUT! OUT!
content.concept: Children playing games during a holiday.
content.explanation: Jeet and Babli are having fun playing games like skipping rope and hide and seek. They even made their own ball! 🎉
content.narrative: It was a holiday. Jeet and Babli were playing. They played many games. Both of them skipped the rope. They played hide and seek. Babli and Jeet decided to play ‘bat and ball’. Jeet got ready to throw the ball. Jeet threw the ball. Babli hit the ball. The ball went into Mohit’s garden. But his house was locked. They could not find the ball. So the game stopped. This time Babli picked the ball. Jeet picked the bat. Babli threw the ball. Jeet hit the ball hard. The ball opened up and scattered in the air! Babli jumped and caught a piece of rag. She jumped and shouted, “OUT! OUT!” Babli knew how to make a ball. They got a few rags, paper, and wool. Babli also brought a piece of string. She rolled rags, paper, and wool to make a ball. Then she tied the ball with a string. They liked the new ball. The game started again.

content.subject: english
content.title: Questions about the Story
content.concept: Reflecting on the story through questions.
content.explanation: Let's think about the story and answer some questions together! 🤔

practice_questions.q: What games do you like to play with your friends?
Have you played ‘bat and ball’?

content.subject: english
content.title: Sequence the Story
content.explanation: Let's put the sentences in the right order to tell the story correctly! 📚

practice_questions.q: Number the following sentences in their correct order.
practice_questions.a: 1. Jeet got ready to throw the ball. 2. Jeet threw the ball. 3. Babli hit the ball. 4. The ball went into Mohit’s garden. 5. They lost the ball.

content.subject: english
content.title: Completing the Story
content.explanation: Fill in the blanks to complete the story! 📝

practice_questions.q: Write the names of the children in the story.
Write the items that the children used to make a ball.

content.subject: english
content.title: Finding Opposites
content.explanation: Let's find the opposite words! Can you think of what they are? 🔄

practice_questions.q: Write the opposites of the following words.
practice_questions.a: 1. up - down, 2. in - out, 3. open - closed, 4. fat - thin, 5. hot - cold, 6. small - big

content.subject: english
content.title: Gopal Goes to School
content.explanation: Gopal is a boy who goes to school every day. Just like Gopal, you also go to school to learn new things! 📚
examples: I go to school every day.

content.subject: english
content.title: Liking Fruits
content.explanation: We all have favorite fruits! For example, I like to eat mangoes and grapes. 🍇🥭 What about you?

content.subject: english
content.title: Animals on the Mat
content.explanation: Cats and dogs are common pets. They can sit on the mat just like we can sit on a chair! 🐱🐶
examples: The cat sat on the mat.
The dog sat on the mat.

content.subject: english
content.title: Describing a Ball
content.explanation: This ball has yellow and green dots. We can describe objects using colors! ⚽️🌈
examples: This ball has yellow dots on it.
This ball also has green dots on it.

content.subject: english
content.title: Classroom Objects
content.explanation: In our classroom, we have tables and chairs. These are important for learning! 🪑📚
examples: There are tables in my classroom.
There are chairs in my classroom.

content.subject: english
content.title: Enact the Story
content.concept: Engaging in role play to enhance understanding.
content.explanation: Choose a partner and act out the story of Babli and Jeet! Use your imagination! 🎭

content.subject: english
content.title: Learn About Childhood Games
content.explanation: Ask your grandparents or parents about games they played when they were young. Share it in class! 🕹️👵👴

content.subject: english
content.title: It’s Fun to Be This
content.concept: Exploring fun activities through a poem.
content.explanation: This poem talks about fun things we can do like jumping and climbing! Let's read it together! 🌟
content.text:
It’s fun to be this,
It’s fun to be that,
To leap like a lamb,
To climb like a cat.
To hop like a frog,
To swim like a fish,
To trot like a horse,
To fly like a bird.
I can act
Just like them all,
It’s fun to be this,
It’s fun to be that.

content.subject: english
content.title: Guess the Animal Sounds
content.explanation: Listen to the sounds and guess which animal makes them! 🐸🐔🐦

content.subject: english
content.title: If You Were an Animal
content.explanation: If you were a cat, what would you do? Let's share our answers! 🐱💭

practice_questions.q: If you were a cat, what would you do?
practice_questions.a: I would run after a mouse.

practice_questions.q: If you meet an elephant, what would you say?

content.subject: english
content.title: What Can You Do Like an Animal?
content.explanation: What can you do like any animal? Let's share our talents! 🦁✨

practice_questions.q: What can you do like any animal or bird?
practice_questions.a: I can jump like a frog.

practice_questions.q: Name any two animals or birds that can swim?
Name any two animals that live on trees?

content.subject: english
content.title: Make Sentences
content.explanation: Let's make sentences with the words we learned! For example, 'Monkeys climb trees.' 🐒🌳

content.subject: english
content.title: Describe the Picture
content.explanation: Look at the picture and write a few sentences about what you see! 🖼️✍️

content.subject: english
content.title: Matching Animals to Actions
content.explanation: Let's learn what different animals do! 🐴🐑🐍🐿️🐒

practice_questions.q: What does a horse do?
What does a monkey do?

content.subject: english
content.title: Song of I
content.explanation: The letter 'I' is special because it is both a letter and a word! 🎶

content.subject: english
content.title: Song of I
content.explanation: The letter 'I' is special because it is both a letter and a word! 🎶

content.subject: english
content.title: Rangoli Art
content.explanation: Let's draw a beautiful rangoli! 🎨✨ Follow the steps to make it colorful.

content.subject: english
content.title: Onshangla's New Friend
content.concept: Understanding challenges faced by differently abled individuals.
content.explanation: Onshangla learns about a new boy who cannot see and plays a fun game with her mother. 🌹🎉

content.subject: english
content.title: Finding Out Without Seeing
content.concept: Children can learn through different senses.
content.explanation: Even if you can't see, you can learn about things using your other senses! For example, you can smell flowers or hear sounds to understand your surroundings. 🌼👂
examples: Identifying a flower by its smell
Finding out where a sound is coming from

content.subject: english
content.title: Close Your Eyes and Identify
content.explanation: Try to identify things without looking! Close your eyes and think about how you would recognize these items.

practice_questions.q: How would you identify water?
practice_questions.a: By feeling its temperature or listening to its sound.

practice_questions.q: How would you identify a flower?

content.subject: english
content.title: Let Us Draw
content.explanation: Draw things you love! Sharing your drawings with friends is fun and helps you express yourself. 🎨😊

content.subject: english
content.title: Activities with Family
content.explanation: Think about fun things you do with your family and write them down! It helps you remember special moments. 🏡❤️
examples: I like to walk with my grandfather.

practice_questions.q: What do you like to do with your family?
practice_questions.a: I like to play games with my sister.

content.subject: english
content.title: Things I Can Do By Myself
content.explanation: List things you can do all by yourself! It shows how you are growing up. 🌱✨
examples: I can take a bath by myself.

content.subject: english
content.title: Taking Care of Our Eyes
content.explanation: Our eyes are important! Let's learn what is good and not good for them. 👀💖

practice_questions.q: What should you do for your eyes?
What is not good for your eyes?
practice_questions.a: Sitting too close to the TV.

content.subject: english
content.title: Eye Movements
content.explanation: Let's exercise our eyes! Follow the teacher's instructions to keep your eyes healthy. 🏋️‍♂️👁️

content.subject: english
content.title: Learning Vowel Sounds
content.explanation: Let's learn about the vowel sound 'o'! Words like 'ox' and 'orange' start with this sound. 🐂🍊

content.subject: english
content.title: Going Places
content.explanation: Let's have fun with this rhyme about traveling! 🚍✈️
content.text:
Take a bus 
Or take a train,
Take a boat 
Or take a plane,
Take a taxi, 
Take a car,
Maybe near 
Or maybe far,

content.subject: english
content.title: Talking About Travel
content.explanation: Let's talk about our travel experiences! Where have you been? What did you see? 🌍🍕

practice_questions.q: Have you ever traveled by bus or train?
practice_questions.a: Yes, I went to the beach!

content.subject: english
content.title: Exploring Vehicles
content.explanation: Vehicles help us travel from one place to another. They can be on land, in the air, or on water. 🚗✈️🚢

content.subject: english
content.title: Take a Rocket
content.concept: A fun rhyme about traveling to the moon.
content.explanation: This rhyme encourages imagination and fun with travel! 🌙🚀
content.text:
Take a rocket
To the moon,
But be sure
To come back soon.

content.subject: english
content.title: Draw Your Favorite Vehicle
content.explanation: Draw the vehicle that you like the most! It can be real or imaginary. 🎨🚗

content.subject: english
content.title: Sort the Vehicles
content.concept: Classifying vehicles based on their movement.
content.explanation: Sort the vehicles into land, air, and water categories. 🚗✈️🚢

content.subject: english
content.title: Vowel Sound ‘u’
content.explanation: The vowel sound 'u' is found in words like 'hut' and 'umbrella'. Let's practice! 🏠☂️

content.subject: english
content.title: Ravi's Journey
content.concept: A story about a fun journey to school.
content.explanation: Ravi and his friend have an exciting adventure on their way to school! 🏫🌳
content.text: Hello, I am Ravi. I study in Class 2. My friend and I go to school on foot. We pass through the paddy fields, the mango grove and then reach the main road. That is the shortest route we take to school. What fun we have every day!

content.subject: english
content.title: Describe Your Journey
content.explanation: Write about the interesting things you see on your way to school! 📝🌼

practice_questions.q: What interesting things do you see on your way to school?
practice_questions.a: I see trees, birds, and sometimes a dog.

practice_questions.q: Ask your family about a long journey they had.
practice_questions.a: They went to the mountains and used a car.

content.subject: english
content.title: This is My Town
content.concept: Understanding the structure of a town and its components.
content.explanation: In my town, there are many streets, houses, and rooms. Let's explore what we can find in each place! 🏡🚶‍♂️
examples: In my town, there are streets.
In my street, there are houses.

practice_questions.q: What do you see in the picture?
practice_questions.a: Describe the elements in the picture.

practice_questions.q: What is the name of your town/city?
What is the name of your street?

content.subject: english
content.title: Rearranging Jumbled Letters
content.explanation: Let's have fun rearranging letters to make words! 🅰️🔤

content.subject: english
content.title: Make Sentences
content.explanation: Use the words to make your own sentences! 📝✨

practice_questions.q: Make a sentence using the word 'tree'.
Make a sentence using the word 'lizard'.

content.subject: english
content.title: Poem about My Town
content.concept: Understanding the beauty of my town through a poem.
content.explanation: Let's enjoy this lovely poem about the things in our town! 🌸🏙️
content.text:
This is my town.
In my town,
There are streets.
In my street,
There are houses.
In my house,
There are rooms.
In my room,
There is a bed.
On the bed,
There is a basket.
In the basket,
There are some flowers.
Flowers in a basket.
Basket on the bed.
Bed in the room.
Room in the house.
House in the street.
Street in the town.

content.subject: english
content.title: Write Your Home Address
content.explanation: Let's practice writing our home address! 🏠✍️

content.subject: english
content.title: Listen and Encircle
content.concept: Improving listening skills through word recognition.
content.explanation: Listen carefully to the words and encircle them! 👂🔍

content.subject: english
content.title: Spot the Differences
content.explanation: Look closely at the pictures and find the differences! 🔍👀

content.subject: english
content.title: Complete the Picture
content.explanation: Use your imagination to add to the picture! 🎨🖌️

content.subject: english
content.title: A Show of Clouds
content.explanation: When we look up at the sky, we can see different shapes in the clouds. It's fun to imagine what they look like! ☁️✨

practice_questions.q: What do you see in the sky during the day?
practice_questions.a: I see the sun ☀️, birds 🐦, and clouds ☁️.

practice_questions.q: What do you see in the sky at night?
practice_questions.a: I see stars ⭐ and the moon 🌙.

content.subject: english
content.title: Cloud Poem
content.content:
As I lie on my back,
Looking up at the sky.
I see a big white bear,
Looking down at me.
As I lie on my back,
Looking up at the sky.
I see a ship sailing,
On a white sea.
As I lie on my back,
Looking up at the sky.
I see an elephant,
Waving its trunk at me.
As I lie on my back,
Looking up at the sky.
Oh! It’s fun, it’s fun to me,
To see whatever I want to see.

content.subject: english
content.title: Cloud Watching Activity
content.explanation: Sit outside and watch the clouds. What shapes do you see? Share your observations with your class! 🌤️

practice_questions.q: What did you see in the clouds?
What colors can clouds be?
practice_questions.a: Clouds can be white, grey, or even pink at sunset! 🌅

content.subject: english
content.title: Common Sounds
content.explanation: Let's listen to words that have the same sound! Can you hear the 'ck' sound? 🗣️

content.subject: english
content.title: A Curious Fly
content.content: The fly said, “Dear tree, do you know my name?” The tree said, “I don’t know. You may ask the cow sleeping in my shade.”

content.subject: english
content.title: Remembering Names
content.concept: Understanding the importance of names and identity.
content.explanation: Names are special! They help us know who we are. Just like the little fly who forgot his name and asked others for help! 🐝✨
examples: The fly asked the ant, 'Do you know my name?'
The cow suggested asking the grass.

practice_questions.q: How did the fly find his name?
practice_questions.a: The fly remembered his name when he saw the leaf flying.

practice_questions.q: If you forget your name, whom would you ask?
practice_questions.a: I would ask my friends or family.

content.subject: english
content.title: The Fly Who Forgot His Name
content.concept: A story about a fly who forgets his name and seeks help.
content.explanation: Once there was a little fly who couldn't remember his name! He asked an ant, a cow, and grass, but they didn't know. Finally, he remembered his name when he saw a leaf flying! 🐞🍃

content.subject: english
content.title: Animal Sounds
content.concept: Learning to recognize and imitate animal sounds.
content.explanation: Can you make sounds like animals? Let's have fun! 🐶🐱

content.subject: english
content.title: What Do You Hear?
content.concept: Practicing listening skills by identifying sounds.
content.explanation: Close your eyes and listen carefully. What sounds can you hear? 🌳👂

content.subject: english
content.title: Fill in the Blanks
content.concept: Completing sentences based on the story.
content.explanation: Let's fill in the blanks using words from the story! 📝

content.subject: english
content.title: Complete the Story
content.concept: Using creativity to finish a story.
content.explanation: Can you help the cat and cow finish their conversation? 🐱🐄

content.subject: english
content.title: What Does Your Name Mean?
content.explanation: What is the meaning of your name? Let's find out together! 🤔💖

content.subject: english
content.title: Using Descriptive Words
content.explanation: Let's create sentences using the words we have! For example, 'Rohit has a blue blanket.' Now you try!

content.subject: english
content.title: The Crow's Beauty
content.explanation: This poem tells the story of a crow who wants to be beautiful like a peacock. It teaches us that true beauty comes from within! 🌈
content.text:
There once was a crow.
He was black, as you know.
How to be beautiful,
he wanted to know.
Colourful feathers,
he wanted to grow.
He found one peacock
feather.
He stuck it in his tail.
Then he found another,
He picked up all without fail.
He stuck them
in his wings,
Put one on his head.
I am now beautiful,
He danced and said.
The other crows
laughed at him.
“Oh, what a fool you are!
You are beautiful in black.
We love you for
what you are.”

content.subject: english
content.title: Identifying Birds
content.explanation: Can you identify the birds in the box? Write their names in the space provided! 🐦

content.subject: english
content.title: My Favourite Bird
content.explanation: Draw your favorite bird and write its name! Let your imagination fly! 🎨

content.subject: english
content.title: Clapping for Syllables
content.concept: Learning to break words into syllables.
content.explanation: Close your eyes and listen to the words. Clap for each syllable! It makes learning fun! 👏

content.subject: english
content.title: Find the Rhymes
content.explanation: Match the words in Column A with rhyming words in Column B. Let's have some fun with rhymes! 🎶

content.subject: english
content.title: Making Sentences
content.explanation: Use one word from each box to make sentences. Let's see what you can come up with! 📝

content.subject: english
content.title: Anju and Farida's Market Adventure
content.concept: A story about Anju and Farida's trip to the market and a lesson on cleanliness.
content.explanation: Anju and Farida went to the market where they bought some snacks. They learned an important lesson about keeping the environment clean when a monkey showed them how to throw trash in the dustbin. 🌳🐒

content.subject: english
content.title: Learning New Words
content.concept: Understanding new vocabulary related to the story.
content.explanation: Let's learn some new words from the story! These words will help us understand better. 📚✨

content.subject: english
content.title: Discussion Questions
content.concept: Questions to encourage discussion about the story.
content.explanation: Let's think about what we learned from the story! 🤔💭

practice_questions.q: Have you seen people throwing packets or bottles in the park or on the road?
After having biscuits, what do you do with the wrapper?
Imagine you are sitting under a tree. What do you see on the tree?
Is there a dustbin in your school? Do you know where it is?

content.subject: english
content.title: Syllable Clapping Activity
content.concept: An activity to practice listening and syllable recognition.
content.explanation: Close your eyes and listen to your teacher say your name. Clap for each syllable in your name! How many times did you clap? 👏🎉

content.subject: english
content.title: Complete the Sentences
content.concept: Practice writing by completing sentences from the story.
content.explanation: Fill in the blanks with the right words to complete the sentences! 📝✏️

practice_questions.q: Farida and Anju sat on ________________________ under the tree to rest.
There was a monkey sitting ________________________ of the tree.
The two girls had a ________________________ and a bottle of juice.

content.subject: english
content.title: Identify True or False Sentences
content.concept: Practice identifying correct and incorrect sentences.
content.explanation: Read the sentences and put a check (✔️) for correct ones and a cross (❌) for incorrect ones! 📖✅❌

practice_questions.q: Anju and Farida went to their school.
Anju bought a bag and Farida bought a water bottle.
There was a bird on the branch of the tree.
The monkey had a banana.
The monkey threw the banana peel in the dustbin.

content.subject: english
content.title: Share Your Shopping Experience
content.concept: Encouraging students to share their shopping experiences.
content.explanation: Have you ever gone to a nearby shop with your family? Share your experiences with the class! You can also draw or paste pictures of things you liked in the shop. 🛍️🖼️

content.subject: english
content.title: Using Blends in Sentences
content.explanation: Let's use words that start with blends! For example, 'There is a clock in the classroom.' Now, you can create your own sentences using the words below.
examples: There is a cloud in the sky.

content.subject: english
content.title: Little Drops of Water
content.concept: Understanding the importance of small things.
content.explanation: This poem teaches us that little things, like drops of water and acts of kindness, can make a big difference! 🌊❤️

content.subject: english
content.title: Importance of Water
content.explanation: Water is essential for life! Let's talk about all the ways we use water every day. 💧

content.subject: english
content.title: Color the Birds and Plants
content.explanation: Listen carefully to the instructions and color the birds, leaves, and plants as you like! 🎨

content.subject: english
content.title: Make Earth Beautiful
content.explanation: Draw something that you think would make our Earth a more beautiful place! 🌍✨

content.subject: english
content.title: Match the Words from the Poem
content.explanation: Match the words in Column A with Column B to complete the phrases from the poem! 📝

content.subject: english
content.title: Complete the Sentences
content.concept: Using context clues to fill in missing words.
content.explanation: Fill in the blanks using the words from the box! For example, 'A ____________ of milk.' 🥛

content.subject: english
content.title: Rearrange the Words
content.explanation: Rearrange the words to make proper sentences. Remember to start with a capital letter and end with a full stop! 🗣️

content.subject: english
content.title: Touch and Feel Project
content.explanation: Ask your family to let you touch and feel different things in the kitchen. What do they feel like? 🍚🥔

content.subject: english
content.title: We Are All Indians
content.concept: Understanding diversity in languages and cultures across India.
content.explanation: In India, we have many languages and cultures! Each person can share where they come from and what they like. 🌏✨
examples: Ramu comes from Gujarat and speaks Gujarati.
Meenakshi comes from Tamil Nadu and speaks Tamil.

practice_questions.q: How old are you?
What is your name?
Where do you study?

content.subject: english
content.title: Fill in the Blanks
content.explanation: Let's practice asking and answering questions about ourselves! 🤔💬
examples: I am Badal. I come from Bengal. I like painting and music.
I am Neerja. I come from Uttar Pradesh. I have seen the Taj Mahal.

practice_questions.q: What language do you speak?
Which tree do you like?

content.subject: english
content.title: New Words
content.concept: Learning new words related to culture and activities.
content.explanation: Let's learn some new words that help us talk about our cultures! 📚🌟
examples: Puppet: A toy that can be moved.

content.subject: english
content.title: Let Us Speak
content.explanation: Let's practice what we say in different situations! 🗣️😊

practice_questions.q: What do you say when you want to enter the classroom?
practice_questions.a: Excuse me, may I come in?

practice_questions.q: What do you say when you need a pencil?

content.subject: english
content.title: What do you like the most?
content.explanation: Let's draw something we love! 🎨 What makes you happy at home? Is it your favorite toy, a pet, or something else?
examples: A drawing of my favorite toy car 🚗
A drawing of my pet dog 🐶

content.subject: english
content.title: Frame Sentences
content.explanation: Let's make sentences! 🗣️ For example, if we have the words 'bright', 'sunny', and 'day', we can say: 'It was a bright sunny day.' Now it's your turn!
examples: It was a bright sunny day. My brother went to buy bread. He crossed the bridge to reach the shop.

content.subject: english
content.title: Discuss with your teacher
content.concept: Answering questions about India and festivals.
content.explanation: Let's talk about our country and festivals! 🇮🇳 Can you answer these questions?

practice_questions.q: What do we call the people who live in India?
Name the festival of colours.
Which festival do you like the most? Why?
practice_questions.a: I like Diwali because of the lights and sweets!

content.subject: english
content.title: Rearrange the letters
content.explanation: Can you solve these puzzles? 🧩 Rearrange the letters to find the names of languages!

practice_questions.q: What language is INHDI?
What language is GNIELHS?
What language is RUUD?
What language is ELUTGU?

content.subject: english
content.title: Use 'but' to join sentences
content.explanation: Let's join sentences! ✍️ For example, 'I like to eat mangoes.' and 'I don’t like to eat apples.' can be joined as 'I like to eat mangoes but I don’t like to eat apples.' Now, try these!

practice_questions.q: Join these sentences: I like milk. I don’t like tea.
practice_questions.a: I like milk but I don’t like tea.

practice_questions.q: Join these sentences: My grandfather is old. He is strong.
practice_questions.a: My grandfather is old but he is strong.

practice_questions.q: Join these sentences: I wanted to go out. I was too tired.
practice_questions.a: I wanted to go out but I was too tired.

content.subject: english
content.title: The Mystery Object
content.concept: Exploring curiosity and learning about history.
content.explanation: In a classroom, students find an unusual object. They wonder what it is and learn about Mahatma Gandhi! 🌟

content.subject: english
content.title: Understanding the Charkha
content.concept: The charkha symbolizes self-reliance and independence.
content.explanation: Mahatma Gandhi used the charkha to spin thread and promote local crafts. 🌱 This handkerchief is made from the thread spun on the charkha!
examples: A handkerchief made from spun thread.

practice_questions.q: What did Mahatma Gandhi use the charkha for?
What does the charkha symbolize?

content.subject: english
content.title: Matching Fruits to Trees
content.concept: Understanding the connection between fruits and their trees.
content.explanation: Can you match the fruits to the trees they grow on? 🍌🍏 Let's learn about nature!
examples: Banana grows on a banana tree.
Apple grows on an apple tree.

practice_questions.q: Which tree does the coconut grow on?
Where do grapes grow?

content.subject: english
content.title: The Festival of Flowers
content.concept: Celebrating the arrival of Spring with flowers.
content.explanation: During Phool Dei, children collect flowers and offer them to elders. 🌸 They receive rice and jaggery in return!

practice_questions.q: What do children collect during Phool Dei?
What do elders give in return?

content.subject: english
content.title: Celebrating Sharad Poornima
content.concept: Understanding the significance of Sharad Poornima.
content.explanation: Sharad Poornima is celebrated on a full moon night in September-October. 🌕 It marks the end of the monsoon season.

practice_questions.q: When is Sharad Poornima celebrated?
What does it mark the end of?

content.subject: english
content.title: The Festival of Sikkim
content.explanation: Pang Lhabsol is celebrated in Sikkim to worship Mt Kanchenjunga. 🏔️ People dance and celebrate together!

practice_questions.q: What do people worship during Pang Lhabsol?
When is this festival celebrated?

content.subject: english
content.title: The Tamil Monsoon Festival
content.concept: Understanding the significance of Aadi Perukku.
content.explanation: Aadi Perukku is a Tamil festival where people worship the river Cauvery. 🌊 Farmers pray for a good harvest!

practice_questions.q: What do people worship during Aadi Perukku?
What do farmers pray for?

content.subject: english
content.title: Discovering Folk Songs
content.concept: Learning about local folk songs from family.
content.explanation: Ask your family about a folk song they used to sing. 🎶 Learn it in your mother tongue and share it with the class!

content.subject: english
content.title: Understanding Holistic Development
content.concept: Holistic development nurtures all aspects of a child's growth.
content.explanation: Holistic development means helping children grow in many ways: physically, emotionally, socially, and mentally. 🌱 For example, playing with friends helps them learn to share and be kind!

practice_questions.q: What does holistic development mean?
practice_questions.a: It means growing in many ways like physically and emotionally.

practice_questions.q: Can you name one way to help a child grow?
practice_questions.a: Playing with them helps them learn!

content.subject: english
content.title: What is the Foundational Stage?
content.concept: The Foundational Stage is crucial for early education.
content.explanation: The Foundational Stage is when children aged 3-8 learn important skills. 📚 This stage helps them get ready for school and life! It's like planting seeds for a big tree! 🌳

practice_questions.q: What age does the Foundational Stage cover?
Why is this stage important?
practice_questions.a: It helps children learn skills for school and life!

content.subject: english
content.title: Understanding NEP 2020
content.concept: NEP 2020 focuses on improving early education.
content.explanation: The National Education Policy 2020 is a plan to help children learn better in their early years. 🎓 It aims to make education fun and effective! 🌟

practice_questions.q: What does NEP stand for?
What is one goal of NEP 2020?

content.subject: english
content.title: Play-Based Learning
content.concept: Learning through play is essential for children.
content.explanation: Learning isn't just about books! 📚 It's about playing, exploring, and having fun while learning new things! 🎉

practice_questions.q: What is play-based learning?
practice_questions.a: Learning through fun activities and games!

content.subject: english
content.title: Five Domains of Learning
content.concept: There are five important areas of learning.
content.explanation: We learn about our body, feelings, thinking, language, and culture! 🌍 Each part helps us grow in different ways! 🌱

practice_questions.q: Name one domain of learning.

content.subject: english
content.title: Connecting Home and School
content.concept: What we learn at home is important in school.
content.explanation: Your experiences at home help you learn better in school! 🏠➡️🏫 Share your stories and ideas! 💬

practice_questions.q: Why is it important to share home experiences?
practice_questions.a: It helps us learn from each other!

content.subject: english
content.title: Making Learning Fun for Everyone
content.concept: Learning should be for everyone, no matter where they come from.
content.explanation: Everyone has different stories and backgrounds! 🌈 Let's celebrate them in our learning! 🎊

practice_questions.q: How can we make learning inclusive?
practice_questions.a: By sharing and respecting everyone's stories!

content.subject: english
content.title: Thinking Questions
content.concept: Asking questions helps us think better.
content.explanation: Let's use our brains! 🧠 Asking questions makes us smarter! 🤔
examples: What happens if we don't water plants? 🌱❓
Why do we need to share? 🤝❓

practice_questions.q: Why is asking questions important?
practice_questions.a: It helps us learn and understand more!

content.subject: english
content.title: Importance of Language
content.concept: Language is a key medium for communication and development.
content.explanation: Language helps us express our thoughts and feelings. It is important for learning and understanding the world around us! 🌍✨

practice_questions.q: Why is language important?
practice_questions.a: It helps us communicate and learn!

content.subject: english
content.title: Learning in Our Own Language
content.concept: Learning is easier in our home language.
content.explanation: When we learn in a language we know well, like our mother tongue, we understand things better! 🏠💬

practice_questions.q: What is your home language?

content.subject: english
content.title: Developing Oral Skills
content.concept: Building strong speaking skills is essential.
content.explanation: Talking clearly and using good words helps us share our ideas! 🗣️💡
examples: Sharing a story about your day

practice_questions.q: How can we improve our speaking skills?

content.subject: english
content.title: Introducing Writing
content.concept: Writing helps us express our thoughts on paper.
content.explanation: Writing is like telling a story, but we do it with letters! ✍️📖
examples: Writing a letter to a friend

practice_questions.q: What do you like to write about?
practice_questions.a: I like to write about my adventures!

content.subject: english
content.title: Learning is Fun
content.full_text:
Learning is fun, oh yes indeed! 🌟
With stories and games, we plant the seed.
We talk and we write, we sing and we play,
In our world of language, we learn every day! 🎉

content.subject: english
content.title: Learning Through Play
content.concept: Learning is more fun with activities!
content.explanation: We learn best when we play and explore! 🎈🤹‍♂️

practice_questions.q: What games do you like to play while learning?

content.subject: english
content.title: Holistic Language Learning
content.concept: Language learning involves thinking, listening, observing, speaking, reading, imagining, and writing.
content.explanation: Learning a language is like going on an adventure! You get to think, listen, and even draw! 🎨 You can talk about pictures and read stories. It's all about having fun while learning! 🌟

content.subject: english
content.title: Understanding Curricular Goals
content.concept: Curricular goals help guide language learning activities.
content.explanation: Curricular goals are like treasure maps that show us how to learn better! 🗺️ They help us listen to songs, understand rhymes, and use new words every day! 🎶

content.subject: english
content.title: Let Us Speak
content.concept: Encouraging conversation builds confidence in speaking English.
content.explanation: Talking is super fun! 🗣️ When we practice speaking, we become better at sharing our thoughts and ideas. Let's chat and have fun! 🎉

practice_questions.q: What is your favorite color?
Can you name three animals?
practice_questions.a: Sure! I can name a cat, dog, and elephant!

content.subject: english
content.title: Colour the Hut
content.concept: Following instructions helps in understanding tasks.
content.explanation: Instructions are like clues! 🕵️‍♂️ When we follow them, we can complete fun tasks like coloring! 🎨 Let's see how well we can listen!
examples: Draw a sun above the hut

content.subject: english
content.title: My Bicycle
content.concept: Enjoying time with friends and having fun.
content.explanation: This story is about a child who loves riding their bicycle and playing games with friends! 🚴‍♂️ It's all about joy and friendship! 🤗
content.narrative: Once upon a time, there was a child who loved riding their bicycle. Every day after school, they would ride around the park, feeling the wind on their face. They would also invite friends to play games like bat-ball and gilli-danda. Together, they laughed and created wonderful memories!

content.subject: english
content.title: Little Drops of Water
content.concept: A poem reflecting Indian values of cooperation.
content.explanation: This poem teaches us about the importance of working together and living in harmony. 🌊🤝
content.text:
Little drops of water,
Little grains of sand,
Make the mighty ocean,
And the pleasant land.
Thus the little minutes,
Humble though they be,
Make the mighty ages
Of eternity.

content.subject: english
content.title: Belonging to Home and Beyond
content.explanation: This lesson helps us feel connected to our home and community. 🏡🌍
examples: My town has parks and schools.
I feel safe in my neighborhood.

practice_questions.q: What makes you feel at home?
practice_questions.a: My family and friends make me feel at home.

content.subject: english
content.title: Travel and Modes of Transport
content.explanation: This lesson talks about how we go to school and other places. 🚍🚴‍♂️
examples: My mom drives me in the car.

practice_questions.q: How do you go to school?

content.subject: english
content.title: Poem about Observation
content.explanation: This poem encourages us to look at the clouds and use our imagination. ☁️✨
content.text:
A Show of Clouds
Look at the clouds in the sky,
They float and dance, oh so high!
Some are fluffy, some are gray,
They change their shapes throughout the day.

content.subject: english
content.topic: The Fly that Forgot its Name
content.title: Story of a Forgetful Fly
content.explanation: This story is about a fly that forgets its name and goes on an adventure to find it. 🐝✨
content.text: Once upon a time, there was a little fly who forgot its name. It buzzed around asking everyone, 'What is my name?' It met a bee, a butterfly, and even a spider. Each told it a different name. Finally, the fly realized it was special just the way it was!

content.subject: english
content.title: Poem about Self-Esteem
content.explanation: This poem teaches us to appreciate ourselves and be proud of who we are. 🐦💖
content.text:
The Crow
A crow sat on a tree,
Feeling proud and free.
With feathers black and bright,
It sang with all its might.

content.subject: english
content.title: Story about Hygiene
content.explanation: This story shows how a smart monkey learns to keep itself and its surroundings clean. 🐒🧼
content.text: Once there was a smart monkey who loved to play. One day, it noticed that its home was messy. The monkey decided to clean up and taught its friends the importance of being tidy and taking care of their surroundings.

content.subject: english
content.title: Regional and National Identity
content.explanation: This lesson teaches us about being proud of our country and respecting our differences. 🇮🇳🤝
examples: People from various cultures live in harmony.

practice_questions.q: What does unity in diversity mean?
practice_questions.a: It means people from different backgrounds live together peacefully.

content.subject: english
content.title: Play about Mahatma Gandhi
content.concept: Learning about historical figures and their contributions.
content.explanation: This short play introduces us to Mahatma Gandhi and the significance of the Charkha in India's history. 🎭🧵
content.text: In this play, children act out the story of Mahatma Gandhi and how he used the Charkha to promote self-reliance and unity among people.

content.subject: english
content.title: My Bicycle
content.concept: Understanding the joy of riding a bicycle.
content.explanation: Riding a bicycle is fun and helps us stay active! 🚴‍♂️ Imagine feeling the wind on your face as you pedal fast down the street.
examples: Riding with friends in the park
Learning to balance on two wheels

practice_questions.q: What do you like about riding a bicycle?
practice_questions.a: I love feeling free and going fast!

content.subject: english
content.title: Picture Reading
content.concept: Interpreting images to tell a story.
content.explanation: Pictures can tell us stories without words! 🖼️ Look at a picture and think about what is happening.

practice_questions.q: What story does this picture tell?
practice_questions.a: It shows friends having fun at a picnic!

content.subject: english
content.title: It is Fun
content.concept: Exploring what makes our world enjoyable.
content.explanation: There are so many fun things in our world! 🌍 From playing games to exploring nature, every day can be an adventure.

practice_questions.q: What is your favorite fun activity?
practice_questions.a: I love going to the amusement park!

content.subject: english
content.title: Seeing without Seeing
content.explanation: Sometimes we can imagine things even if we can't see them! 🌈 Use your imagination to picture your favorite place.
examples: Imagining a beach on a rainy day
Thinking of a favorite book character

practice_questions.q: What can you imagine right now?
practice_questions.a: I can imagine a sunny day at the beach!

content.subject: english
content.title: A Show of Clouds
content.explanation: Clouds can look like many things! ☁️ Sometimes they look like animals or shapes. What do you see in the clouds?
examples: A cloud that looks like a bunny
A cloud that looks like a car

practice_questions.q: What shape do you see in the clouds today?

content.subject: english
content.title: My Name
content.explanation: Our names are special! They tell people who we are. 🌟 What does your name mean?
examples: My name is 'Joy' because I bring happiness!
My name is 'Sam' because it means 'listener'

practice_questions.q: What does your name mean?

content.subject: english
content.title: The Crow
content.concept: Learning about the cleverness of crows.
content.explanation: Crows are very smart birds! 🐦 They can solve problems and find food in clever ways.
content.narrative: Once upon a time, a hungry crow found a pitcher with some water at the bottom. The water was too low for him to reach. So, he dropped pebbles into the pitcher one by one until the water rose high enough for him to drink. What a clever crow!

content.subject: english
content.title: The Smart Monkey
content.explanation: Monkeys are playful and smart! 🐒 They often use their brains to solve problems.
content.narrative: In a jungle, a smart monkey wanted to eat bananas from a tall tree. Instead of climbing, he noticed a stick nearby. He used the stick to knock the bananas down. The other animals were amazed by his cleverness!

content.subject: english
content.title: Little Drops of Water
content.explanation: Water is essential for life! 💧 Let's enjoy this rhyme about little drops of water.
content.content:
Little drops of water, little grains of sand,  
Make the mighty ocean and the pleasant land.  
Thus the little minutes, humble though they be,  
Make the mighty ages of eternity.

content.subject: english
content.title: We are all Indians
content.explanation: We all belong to one country, India! 🇮🇳 Let's celebrate our differences and similarities.

practice_questions.q: What makes India special?
